question_en: What is a period?
question_ne: Period bhaneko ke ho?
response_en: A period is the monthly shedding of the uterine lining.
response_ne: Period bhaneko euta month ma few days lai aaune ragat ho.

question_en: Why do periods happen?
question_ne: Period kina huncha?
response_en: Periods happen as part of the menstrual cycle to prepare the body for pregnancy.
response_ne: Pregnancy ko lagi sharir taiyar garna cycle le period dincha.

question_en: How long do periods last?
question_ne: Period kati din samma huncha?
response_en: Periods usually last 3 to 7 days.
response_ne: Period sajha ma 3 dekhi 7 din samma huncha.

question_en: Is it normal to have pain during periods?
question_ne: Period ma dukhai hunu normal ho?
response_en: Yes, mild to moderate pain is common during periods.
response_ne: Ho, halka ya samanya dukhai ta normal nai ho.

question_en: Why is my period late this month?
question_ne: Yo month mero period kina late bhayo?
response_en: A late period can happen due to several reasons, such as stress, hormonal imbalances, changes in diet or exercise, PCOS, or other health conditions. If your period is consistently late, consider consulting a healthcare professional.
response_ne: Period dhilo hunu ko karan stress, hormonal imbalance, diet ya exercise ma pariwartan, PCOS, ya anya swasthya samasya huna sakchha. Yadi tapaiko period barambar dhilo huncha bhane, doctor sanga paramarsha garnu upyukta huncha.

question_en: What are the signs that my period is about to start?
question_ne: Mero period start hunu lagyo bhanera kasari thapaune?
response_en: Signs that your period is about to start include mood swings, cramps, bloating, tender breasts, acne, and food cravings. These are caused by changes in your body's hormone levels.
response_ne: Tapaiko period suru huna lageko sanket haru mood swing, cramps, bloating, breast tenderness, acne, ra food cravings huna sakchha. Yo tapaiko shareer ma hormonal pariwartan ko karan le hunchha.

question_en: What is PMS?
question_ne: PMS bhaneko ke ho?
response_en: PMS stands for Premenstrual Syndrome, which causes mood swings and other symptoms.
response_ne: PMS bhaneko premenstrual syndrome ho, jasle mood swing garaucha.

question_en: Can I exercise during my period?
question_ne: Period ma exercise garna milcha?
response_en: Yes, light exercise like yoga or walking can help with cramps.
response_ne: Ho, halka exercise jastai yoga ya walking garda huncha.

question_en: Why is my period late?
question_ne: Mero period kina dheelo cha?
response_en: Periods can be late due to stress, diet, or hormonal changes.
response_ne: Tanav, diet, ya hormonal badal le period dheelo huna sakcha.

question_en: What is a menstrual cycle?
question_ne: Menstrual cycle bhaneko ke ho?
response_en: It is the process your body goes through to prepare for pregnancy every month.
response_ne: Menstrual cycle bhaneko pregnancy ko lagi sharir taiyar garna harek month hune cycle ho.

question_en: Can periods make you tired?
question_ne: Period le thakai lagaidincha?
response_en: Yes, loss of blood and hormonal changes can make you tired.
response_ne: Ho, ragat flow bhayepachi ra hormonal changes le garda thakai huna sakcha.

question_en: What is spotting?
question_ne: Spotting bhaneko ke ho?
response_en: Spotting is light bleeding that happens outside of your period.
response_ne: Spotting bhaneko period bahek kehi din halka ragat aune ho.

question_en: Is it safe to use a menstrual cup?
question_ne: Menstrual cup use garna safe cha?
response_en: Yes, menstrual cups are safe and eco-friendly.
response_ne: Ho, menstrual cup safe ra environment friendly ho.

question_en: Is it okay to use tampons?
question_ne: Tampons use garna thik ho?
response_en: Yes, but use it properly.
response_ne: Ho, tara ramro sanga use garnuhos.

question_en: How much blood do we lose during periods?
question_ne: Period ma kati ragat flow huncha?
response_en: On average, about 30-40 ml of blood is lost during a period.
response_ne: Sajha ma 30-40 ml ragat flow huncha.

question_en: Can I swim during my period?
question_ne: Period ma swimming garna milcha?
response_en: Yes, you can swim using tampons or menstrual cups.
response_ne: Ho, tampon ya menstrual cup use garera swimming garna milcha.

question_en: Are irregular periods normal?
question_ne: Irregular period normal ho?
response_en: Occasional irregular periods are normal, but persistent ones need a doctor's advice.
response_ne: Kahile kahi irregular period normal ho, tara barambar bhaye doctor ko salaah linu.

question_en: What is menopause?
question_ne: Menopause bhaneko ke ho?
response_en: Menopause is when periods stop permanently, usually after age 45-50.
response_ne: Menopause bhaneko period permanently rokinu ho, jastai 45-50 barsa pachhi.

question_en: What are sanitary pads?
question_ne: Sanitary pad bhaneko ke ho?
response_en: They are absorbent materials used to collect menstrual blood.
response_ne: Sanitary pad bhaneko ragat soak garna use hune saman ho.

question_en: Why do periods smell sometimes?
question_ne: Period ko smell kina aauchha?
response_en: It could be due to bacteria or menstrual products not being changed on time.
response_ne: Bacteria ya timely pad change nagarda smell auna sakcha.

question_en: What are tampons?
question_ne: Tampon bhaneko ke ho?
response_en: Tampons are absorbent materials inserted into the vagina to collect menstrual blood.
response_ne: Tampon bhaneko vagina vitra ragat soak garna halne saman ho.

question_en: How to track my periods?
question_ne: Mero period kasari track garne?
response_en: You can use a period tracker app or a calendar to note the start and end dates.
response_ne: Period tracker app ya calendar ma suru ra antim din lekhnus ani track garnus.

question_en: Can sexual activity affect my period?
question_ne: Sexual activity le period ma asar parcha?
response_en: Yes, sexual activity can sometimes impact hormonal changes and affect your period.
response_ne: Ho, sexual activity le hormonal changes garayera period ma asar garna sakcha.

question_en: Can stress affect my period?
question_ne: Tanav le period ma asar parcha?
response_en: Yes, stress can delay or change your period.
response_ne: Ho, tanav le period dheelo ya change garna sakcha.

question_en: What does different blood color during periods mean?
question_ne: Period ma rakt ko rang alag bhayeko bhane ke ho?
response_en: Different blood color can indicate hormonal changes, diet, or health issues, such as lighter or darker bleeding.
response_ne: Period ko rakt ko rang ko badal ko artha hormonal change, diet, ya health samasya huna sakcha.

question_en: What causes period pain?
question_ne: Period pain kina huncha?
response_en: Period pain is caused by uterine contractions.
response_ne: Uterine contractions le period ma dukhai huncha.

question_en: Is irregular period normal?
question_ne: Irregular period normal ho?
response_en: Yes, but consult a doctor if it's frequent.
response_ne: Ho, tara dherai samasya bhaye doctor sanga salah linuhos.

question_en: What is a heavy period?
question_ne: Heavy period bhaneko ke ho?
response_en: Heavy periods involve excessive bleeding.
response_ne: Heavy period bhaneko dherai ragat bagne ho.

question_en: How to know if my period is late?
question_ne: Late period kasari thaha pau?
response_en: If it's more than 5 days late, check.
response_ne: 5 din banda dherai late bhaye check garnuhos.

question_en: What are periods?
question_ne: Periods ke ho?
response_en: Periods are part of the menstrual cycle where the uterus sheds its lining.
response_ne: Periods bhaneko uterus ko lining shed hune process ho.

question_en: Why do periods happen?
question_ne: Periods kina hunchha?
response_en: Periods happen due to hormonal changes in the body.
response_ne: Hormonal changes le garda periods hunchha.

question_en: Is it normal to have irregular periods?
question_ne: Aniyamit periods normal ho?
response_en: Yes, occasional irregular periods are normal, but frequent irregularities should be checked by a doctor.
response_ne: Ho, kahilekahi aniyamit periods normal ho, tara barambar hune ho bhane doctor lai dekhaunu parcha

question_en: What should I do if my period is late?
question_ne: Mero period late bhaye ke garne?
response_en: If your period is late, consider stress, hormonal changes, or consult a doctor.
response_ne: Period late bhaye stress ra hormonal change le garda bhako huna sakcha, wa doctor sanga paramarsha garnu parcha.

question_en: Can stress delay periods?
question_ne: Stress le period delay garna sakchha?
response_en: Yes, stress can delay periods by affecting hormonal balance.
response_ne: Ho, stress le hormonal balance bigarera period delay huna sakchha.

question_en: What should I do if I miss a period?
question_ne: Period miss bhaye ke garne?
response_en: If you miss a period and are not pregnant, consult a doctor for advice.
response_ne: Period miss bhaye pregnancy check garnu, wa doctor sanga paramarsha garnu.

question_en: Why do periods cause mood swings?
question_ne: Periods le mood swing kina garcha?
response_en: Hormonal changes during periods affect mood.
response_ne: Hormonal changes le mood swing garna sakcha.

question_en: Why do I feel tired during my period?
question_ne: Periods ma thakai kina lagchha?
response_en: Feeling tired is common due to hormonal changes and blood loss.
response_ne: Hormonal changes ra ragat ko karan le thakai lagchha.

question_en: How often should I change a pad or tampon?
question_ne: Pad wa tampon kati bela change garne?
response_en: You should change your pad or tampon every 4 to 6 hours.
response_ne: Pad wa tampon 4 dekhi 6 ghantama change garnu parcha.

question_en: What is the normal cycle length?
question_ne: Normal cycle length kati din ko ho?
response_en: A normal cycle is 21 to 35 days.
response_ne: Normal cycle 21 dekhi 35 din samma huncha.

question_en: What are the best products for managing periods?
question_ne: Periods manage garna best product ke ho?
response_en: Pads, tampons, menstrual cups, and period underwear are good options.
response_ne: Pads, tampons, menstrual cups, ra period underwear ramro options ho periods manage garna.

question_en: Can periods stop suddenly?
question_ne: Periods achanak banda huna sakchha?
response_en: Yes, periods can stop due to pregnancy, stress, or health conditions.
response_ne: Ho, pregnancy, stress, wa aru health condition le garda period achanak banda huna sakchha.

question_en: How can I reduce period cramps?
question_ne: Period ko cramps kasari kam garne?
response_en: Use a heating pad and stay hydrated.
response_ne: Heating pad use garnuhos ani pani dherai piunuhos.

question_en: What causes period cramps?
question_ne: Period ko dukhai kina hunchha?
response_en: Period cramps are caused by the uterus contracting to shed its lining.
response_ne: Uterus ko lining shed garna uterus le contract garcha jasle garda period ko dukhai hunchha.

question_en: Are there any home remedies for cramps?
question_ne: Cramps ka lagi gharelu upaya ke ho?
response_en: Try ginger tea or a hot compress.
response_ne: Ginger tea ya hot compress try garnuhos.

question_en: Why are my period cramps worse than usual?
question_ne: Mero period ko cramps kina pahila bhanda dherai chha?
response_en: Severe cramps may be caused by hormonal imbalances, stress, or medical conditions like endometriosis. Consider consulting a doctor if the pain is intense.
response_ne: Dherai severe cramps hormone imbalance, stress, ya endometriosis jasto medical condition le huna sakcha. Dukhai dherai intense bhaye doctor sanga paramarsha garnuhos.

question_en: What is the best pain relief for cramps?
question_ne: Cramps ko lagi best pain relief ke ho?
response_en: Over-the-counter painkillers like ibuprofen work well.
response_ne: Ibuprofen jasto over-the-counter painkillers ramro huncha.

question_en: What can I do for period cramps?
question_ne: Period ko pet dukhai kasari thik garne?
response_en: You can use a hot water bag, drink warm water, or take a pain reliever.
response_ne: Garam paani khanu, hot water bag use garnu, ya painkiller lina saknu huncha.

question_en: How can I relieve period cramps?
question_ne: Mero period ko cramps kasari kam garna sakchhu?
response_en: You can relieve period cramps by applying heat to your abdomen, doing light exercise, drinking warm water, or taking pain relievers if necessary.
response_ne: Tapaaile cramps kam garna pet ma heating pad lagauna, halko exercise garna, taato paani khana, ya avashyak parne bela ma dukhai kam garne ausadhi khana saknu huncha.

question_en: Does drinking water reduce period cramps?
question_ne: Pani khayepachi period ko cramps kam huncha?
response_en: Yes, drinking more water can reduce bloating, which may help relieve cramps by improving hydration and muscle relaxation.
response_ne: Ho, dherai paani khayepachi bloating kam hunchha, jasle hydration ra muscle relaxation improve garera cramps kam garna sakcha.

question_en: Are cramps normal during periods?
question_ne: Period ko bela cramps hunu normal ho?
response_en: Yes, mild to moderate cramps are normal during periods, but severe cramps may require medical attention.
response_ne: Ho, halko dekhi madhyam cramps period ko bela normal ho, tara dherai severe cramps bhaye ta doctor sanga consult garna parcha.

question_en: Can painkillers help with period cramps?
question_ne: Dukhai kam garne ausadhi le period ko cramps kam garna sakcha?
response_en: Yes, over-the-counter painkillers like ibuprofen or paracetamol can relieve period cramps. But consult a doctor if pain persists.
response_ne: Ho, ibuprofen ya paracetamol jasto over-the-counter ausadhi le period ko cramps kam garna sakcha. Tara, dukhai lamo samay samma rahane ho bhane doctor sanga paramarsha garnuhos.

question_en: Why am I having cramps even though my period is not here?
question_ne: Mero period aayeko chhaina tara pani malai cramps kina hunchha?
response_en: Cramps outside your period can be caused by ovulation, hormonal changes, stress, or digestive issues. If the pain is severe or frequent, it might be good to consult a doctor.
response_ne: Period nabhayepani cramps ovulation, hormonal parivartan, tanav, wa pacho sambandhi samasyale huna sakchha. Dukhai dherai cha ya barambar aayo bhane, doctor sanga paramarsha garna ramro huncha.

question_en: I have cramps but my period just ended. Is this normal?
question_ne: Mero period bharkhar sakiyo, tara malai cramps cha. Yo normal ho?
response_en: Cramps after a period may be caused by uterine contractions or leftover hormonal effects. If it persists, consult a doctor.
response_ne: Period sakepachi ko cramps bachhaeko hormonal asar ya uterus ko contraction lehuna sakchha. Yo lambiyera gayo bhane, doctor sanga paramarsha garna parne hunchha.

question_en: I have severe cramps but no bleeding. Is it a sign of a problem?
question_ne: Ragat aayeko chhaina tara dherai cramps cha. Yo samasya ko sanket ho?
response_en: Severe cramps without bleeding could be linked to ovarian cysts, endometriosis, or other medical conditions. It's best to consult a gynecologist.
response_ne: Ragat aayeko chhaina tara dherai cramps huda ovarian cysts, endometriosis, wa aru samasya sambandhit huna sakchha. Gynecologist sanga paramarsha garna utkrishta huncha.

question_en: Do cramps mean I'm about to get my period?
question_ne: Cramps aayepachi period auna lako ho bhanne ho?
response_en: Yes, cramps can be an early sign that your period is about to start. It's caused by the uterus preparing to shed its lining.
response_ne: Ho, cramps tapaaiko period aune sanket huna sakchha. Yo uterus le aafno wall ko layer fyalna laagne taiyari gardaa hunchha.

question_en: Can I get cramps from eating too much junk food?
question_ne: Dherai junk food khaye pachhi cramps hun sakchha?
response_en: Yes, junk food can cause bloating and indigestion, leading to cramps. Eating fiber-rich food and staying hydrated can help.
response_ne: Ho, junk food le pet fulne ra pachna garo parne bhayekale cramps hun sakchha. Fiber bhako khana khanu ra pani dherai piunuhos.

question_en: I get cramps after exercising. Is this normal?
question_ne: Exercise garepachi cramps aauchha. Yo normal ho?
response_en: Cramps after exercise are often caused by dehydration or muscle fatigue. Drink water and stretch properly before and after exercising.
response_ne: Exercise garepachi cramps aunu dehydration wa muscle thakai le huna sakchha. Exercise agadi ra pachhi thik tarikale tankine yoga wa exercise garera ra dherai paani piune garnda upayogi hunchha.

question_en: Why do I have cramps on one side of my lower abdomen?
question_ne: Mero pet ko euta side ma matra cramps kina aauchha?
response_en: Cramps on one side could be due to ovulation (mittelschmerz) or an ovarian cyst. If the pain becomes severe, seek medical advice.
response_ne: Ek paksha ma cramps aunu ovulation (mittelschmerz) ya ovarian cyst le huna sakchha. Dukhai dherai bhayo bhane, doctor sanga consult garnuhos.

question_en: Can irregular periods cause cramps at unexpected times?
question_ne: Aniyamit period le achanak cramps auna sakchha?
response_en: Yes, irregular periods can cause cramps at unexpected times due to hormonal fluctuations. Tracking your cycle can help you understand the pattern.
response_ne: Ho, aniyamit period le hormonal utar-chadhav ko karan akasmaat cramps auna sakchha. Aafno cycle track garnu bhayo bhane, pattern bujhna madat garna sakchha.

question_en: I have cramps and nausea but no period. Why?
question_ne: Cramps cha ra man kharab cha, tara period aayeko chhaina. Kina?
response_en: Cramps with nausea could be a sign of ovulation, pregnancy, or digestive issues. If you suspect pregnancy, take a test to be sure.
response_ne: Cramps ra man kharab hunu ovulation, pregnancy, ya pachilo sambandhi samasya ko sanket huna sakchha. Pregnancy ko asanka bhaye test garera nischit hunu upayogi hunchha.

question_en: I have sharp cramps after intercourse. Is it normal?
question_ne: Intercourse pachhi dherai cramps aauchha. Yo normal ho?
response_en: Mild cramps after intercourse can be normal, but sharp or intense pain could indicate an underlying condition like ovarian cysts or endometriosis. Consult a doctor if it persists.
response_ne: Intercourse pachhi halka cramps normal ho, tara dherai dukhai bhaye ovarian cysts ya endometriosis jasta samasya ko sanket huna sakchha. Yo jari rahe bhane doctor sanga paramarsha garna upayogi hunchha.

question_en: Can dehydration cause cramps?
question_ne: Dehydration le cramps huna sakchha?
response_en: Yes, dehydration can cause muscle cramps, including in the lower abdomen. Drink plenty of water to stay hydrated.
response_ne: Ho, dehydration le muscle cramps huna sakchha, jasma lower abdomen pani samel hunchha. Ramro sanga hydrated huda ra dherai paani piune garda upayogi hunchha.

question_en: What is PCOS?
question_ne: PCOS bhane ko ke ho?
response_en: PCOS (Polycystic Ovary Syndrome) is a hormonal disorder where ovaries produce excess male hormones, leading to irregular periods, weight gain, and cyst formation.
response_ne: PCOS (Polycystic Ovary Syndrome) ek hormonal disorder ho, jasma ovary le male hormone adhik matra ma utpadan garcha, jasle irregular periods, wajan badhne, ra cyst banna jasto samasya uthisakcha.

question_en: What are the symptoms of PCOS?
question_ne: PCOS ko lakshan haru ke ke ho?
response_en: Symptoms of PCOS include irregular periods, weight gain, acne, excess facial hair, and difficulty getting pregnant
response_ne: PCOS ko lakshan haru irregular periods, wajan badhne, muhans (acne), anuhar ma adhik kapaal, ra garbha dharan garna garho huna sakchha.

question_en: Can PCOS be cured?
question_ne: PCOS thik huna sakcha?
response_en: PCOS cannot be fully cured, but it can be managed through lifestyle changes, medications, and hormone therapy.
response_ne: PCOS pura thik garna ta sakidaina, tara lifestyle change, ausadhi, ra hormone therapy le yaslai niyantran ma rakhna sakinchha.

question_en: How is PCOS diagnosed?
question_ne: PCOS kasari diagnose garincha?
response_en: PCOS is diagnosed through medical history, physical examination, ultrasound, and hormone blood tests.
response_ne: PCOS, medical history, sharirik parikshan, ultrasound, ra hormone blood test bata diagnose garincha.

question_en: Can I get pregnant if I have PCOS?
question_ne: Mero PCOS bhaye pani ma pregnant huna sakchu?
response_en: Yes, you can still get pregnant with PCOS, but it might require treatment or lifestyle changes to support ovulation.
response_ne: Ho, PCOS bhaye pani tapaaile garbha dharan garna saknuhuncha, tara ovulation support garna ausadhi ya lifestyle change ko avashyakta huna sakchha.

question_en: What is PCOD?
question_ne: PCOD bhane ko ke ho?
response_en: PCOD (Polycystic Ovarian Disease) is a condition where ovaries release immature eggs, leading to cyst formation. It can cause irregular periods, acne, and hair loss.
response_ne: PCOD (Polycystic Ovarian Disease) ma ovaries le pura pakeko anda chhodaudaina, jasle cyst banna sakcha. Yasle irregular periods, acne, ra kapaal jharne jasto samasya lyauchha.

question_en: What's the difference between PCOS and PCOD?
question_ne: Pcos ra pcod ma antar k cha?
response_en: PCOS is a hormonal disorder caused by excess androgens, while PCOD is a condition where ovaries release immature eggs. PCOS is more severe than PCOD
response_ne: PCOS ek hormonal disorder ho, jun androgens (male hormone) ko adhikta le huncha, jaba ki PCOD ma ovaries le paka naako anda release garcha. PCOS, PCOD bhanda dherai severe huncha.

question_en: What are the symptoms of PCOD?
question_ne: PCOD ko lakshan haru ke ke ho?
response_en: Symptoms of PCOD include irregular periods, abdominal pain, weight gain, and hormonal imbalance.
response_ne: PCOD ko lakshan haru irregular periods, pet dukhai, wajan badhne, ra hormone imbalance huna sakchha.

question_en: Is PCOD a serious problem?
question_ne: PCOD serious problem ho?
response_en: PCOD is not as serious as PCOS and can often be managed with lifestyle changes, but it may affect fertility if not managed properly.
response_ne: PCOD, PCOS jasto serious ta hoina, tara yo lifestyle change le niyantran ma rakhna sakchha. Thik tarikale niyantran nagare fertility ma asar parna sakchha.

question_en: What is ovulation?
question_ne: Ovulation bhaneko ke ho?
response_en: Ovulation is the release of an egg from the ovary, usually occurring in the middle of your menstrual cycle.
response_ne: Ovulation bhaneko andebij ko ovary bata release hunu ho, jasma menstrual cycle ko madhya ma garincha.

question_en: Can the app predict my ovulation date?
question_ne: App le mero ovulation date predict garna sakcha?
response_en: Yes, the app uses your cycle history to estimate your ovulation date.
response_ne: Ho, app le timro cycle history ko adhar ma ovulation date ko estimation garcha.

question_en: How accurate are ovulation predictions in the app?
question_ne: App ma ovulation predictions kati accurate huncha?
response_en: The accuracy depends on how consistently you log your cycle data and symptoms.
response_ne: Ovulation prediction ko accuracy timile cycle data ani symptoms kasto consistency le log garchau bhandaparda huncha.

question_en: Can I track ovulation symptoms in the app?
question_ne: App ma ovulation symptoms track garna milcha?
response_en: Yes, you can log symptoms like cervical mucus changes, ovulation pain, or basal body temperature.
response_ne: Ho, timile cervical mucus changes, ovulation pain, ani basal body temperature jasta symptoms log garna sakchau.

question_en: What are signs of ovulation I should log?
question_ne: Ovulation ko ke sanket haru log garna parcha?
response_en: Signs include cervical mucus changes, ovulation pain, and increased basal body temperature.
response_ne: Cervical mucus ma pariwartan, ovulation pain, ani basal body temperature badhnu jasta sanket log garna parcha.

question_en: Can the app identify my fertile window?
question_ne: App le mero fertile window identify garna sakcha?
response_en: Yes, the app estimates your fertile window based on ovulation predictions and cycle data.
response_ne: Ho, app le ovulation predictions ani cycle data ko adhar ma fertile window estimate garcha.

question_en: How can I use the app to plan pregnancy?
question_ne: Pregnancy plan garna app kasari upyog garna sakchhu?
response_en: The app helps identify your most fertile days for better pregnancy planning.
response_ne: App le timro sabai bhanda fertile din haru identify garera pregnancy planning ma madat garcha.

question_en: What should I log to improve ovulation tracking?
question_ne: Ovulation tracking improve garna ke log garna parcha?
response_en: Log your cycle length, cervical mucus, ovulation pain, and basal body temperature.
response_ne: Cycle length, cervical mucus, ovulation pain, ani basal body temperature log garna parcha.

question_en: Does the app notify me when I am ovulating?
question_ne: Ovulation ko bela app le malai notify garcha?
response_en: Yes, the app can send reminders or notifications for predicted ovulation days.
response_ne: Ho, app le predicted ovulation din ko lagi reminders athwa notifications dincha.

question_en: How does the app calculate ovulation?
question_ne: App le ovulation kasari calculate garcha?
response_en: The app uses your menstrual cycle length and patterns to estimate ovulation.
response_ne: App le menstrual cycle ko length ani pattern use garera ovulation calculate garcha.

question_en: Can irregular cycles affect ovulation tracking in the app?
question_ne: Irregular cycles le app ma ovulation tracking ma asar garcha?
response_en: Yes, irregular cycles can make tracking less accurate, but logging detailed data helps.
response_ne: Ho, irregular cycles le tracking accurate hunu ghatdincha, tara detailed data log garera madat hunchha.

question_en: What is the fertile window, and how does the app show it?
question_ne: Fertile window bhaneko ke ho, ani app le kasari dekhauncha?
response_en: The fertile window is the time when you're most likely to conceive, shown by the app based on ovulation predictions.
response_ne: Fertile window bhaneko garbh dharan garna sakne sabai bhanda sambhavana din haru ho, jun app le ovulation prediction ko adhar ma dekhauncha.

question_en: Can I track ovulation if I have PCOS?
question_ne: PCOS bhaeko huda ni ovulation track garna milcha?
response_en: Yes, though it may be less predictable, the app can help track ovulation symptoms over time.
response_ne: Ho, yasle accurate predict nagarna sakla, tara app le symptoms over time track garna madat garcha.

question_en: How does ovulation affect my menstrual cycle?
question_ne: Ovulation le menstrual cycle ma kasto asar garcha?
response_en: Ovulation determines your cycle's length and timing of the fertile window.
response_ne: Ovulation le cycle ko length ani fertile window ko timing determine garcha.

question_en: Can the app predict anovulatory cycles?
question_ne: App le anovulatory cycles predict garna sakcha?
response_en: The app may flag irregular patterns that suggest anovulatory cycles, but medical advice is needed for confirmation.
response_ne: App le irregular patterns flag garera anovulatory cycles ko sanket dincha, tara confirmation ko lagi doctor ko sallaha chahincha.

question_en: What is the role of basal body temperature in ovulation tracking?
question_ne: Ovulation tracking ma basal body temperature ko role kasto huncha?
response_en: A rise in basal body temperature often indicates ovulation, which the app can help you track.
response_ne: Basal body temperature ko badhai ovulation ko sanket ho, jun app le track garna madat garcha.

question_en: Can stress affect ovulation predictions in the app?
question_ne: Stress le app ma ovulation predictions ma asar garcha?
response_en: Yes, stress can delay ovulation, impacting the app's predictions.
response_ne: Ho, stress le ovulation late garna sakcha, jasle app ko predictions ma asar purauchha.

question_en: What is ovulation pain, and can I track it in the app?
question_ne: Ovulation pain bhaneko ke ho, ani app ma yo track garna milcha?
response_en: Ovulation pain is a mild ache in the lower abdomen during ovulation, and it can be logged in the app.
response_ne: Ovulation pain bhaneko ovulation ko belama talo pet ma halka dukhai ho, ani yo app ma log garna milcha.

question_en: Can irregular sleep patterns affect ovulation tracking?
question_ne: Irregular sleep pattern huda ni app le ovulation track garna sakcha?
response_en: Yes, but tracking basal body temperature might be less reliable with irregular sleep.
response_ne: Ho, tara basal body temperature irregular sleep pattern ma kam reliable huna sakcha.

question_en: Can I be pregnant if I miss a period?
question_ne: Mero period miss huda pregnant bhako huna sakchhu?
response_en: Yes, missing a period can be one of the first signs of pregnancy.
response_ne: Ho, period miss bhayeko ma pregnancy ko sign huna sakcha.

question_en: How soon can I know if I'm pregnant?
question_ne: Mero pregnancy ko bare ma kati chhito thaha pauna sakchhu?
response_en: You can take a pregnancy test as soon as you miss your period or after a few days if you suspect pregnancy.
response_ne: Timi period miss garne saath ya kehi din pachhi pregnancy test garera thaha pauna sakchau.

question_en: What are the first signs of pregnancy?
question_ne: Pregnancy ko pahilo sanket k ho?
response_en: The first signs of pregnancy can include a missed period, nausea, and breast tenderness.
response_ne: Pregnancy ko pahilo sanket haru period miss garne, nausea, ra breast tenderness samet huna sakchha.

question_en: How do I know if I'm pregnant without taking a test?
question_ne: Test nagari  kasari thaha paune ki ma pregnant chu?
response_en: You can look for signs like nausea, fatigue, and missed periods to suspect pregnancy.
response_ne: Nausea, fatigue, ra period miss garne jasta sanket haru herera pregnancy ko sanket lagauna sakchau.

question_en: Can I still get my period if I'm pregnant?
question_ne: Pregnant bhayepachi pani period aauna sakcha?
response_en: It is uncommon, but some women may experience light bleeding or spotting during early pregnancy.
response_ne: Yo uncommon ho, tara kehi mahilale pregnancy ko suru ko dauraan light bleeding ya spotting experience garna sakchhan.

question_en: How long after a missed period should I take a pregnancy test?
question_ne: Period miss gare pachhi kati din pachhi pregnancy test garne?
response_en: You should wait at least a week after a missed period before taking a pregnancy test for more accurate results.
response_ne: Period miss gare pachhi accurate result pauna ek hapta jati parkhina parcha.

question_en: What should I do if I think I am pregnant?
question_ne: Ma pregnant chu jasto lagcha bhane ke garne?
response_en: If you suspect pregnancy, take a test and consult a doctor for confirmation.
response_ne: Pregnancy ko shak bhayeko bhaye, test garera doctor sanga parikshan garne.

question_en: Can stress affect a pregnancy test result?
question_ne: Stress le pregnancy test ko result affect garna sakcha?
response_en: No, stress does not affect the results of a pregnancy test.
response_ne: Stress le pregnancy test ko result ma kunai asar gardaina.

question_en: Can I get pregnant right after my period?
question_ne: Mero period ko turuntai pachhi ma pregnant huna sakchhu?
response_en: It's unlikely but possible, as ovulation can occur soon after your period ends.
response_ne: Yo khassai hudaina tara kunai kunai case ma huna sakcha kina bhane ovulation period ko turunta pachhi huna sakchha.

question_en: How accurate are home pregnancy tests?
question_ne: Home pregnancy tests kati ko accurate hunchha?
response_en: Home pregnancy tests are generally accurate if used correctly, around 99% effective.
response_ne: Home pregnancy tests sahi tarikale use garema lagbhag 99% accurate hunchha.

question_en: Can I get pregnant while breastfeeding?
question_ne: Breastfeeding garirako ble pregnant huna sakcha?
response_en: Yes, it is possible to get pregnant while breastfeeding, though it's less likely.
response_ne: Ho, breastfeeding garda pregnant huna sakchha, tara yo less possible cha.

question_en: What are the chances of getting pregnant on your period?
question_ne: Period ko bela pregnant hune chances kati cha?
response_en: The chances are low but not impossible, as sperm can survive in the body for several days.
response_ne: Period ko dauraan pregnant hune chances kam cha tara sambhav cha kina bhane sperm kai din samma body ma survive garna sakcha.

question_en: Can pregnancy cause mood swings?
question_ne: Pregnancy le mood swings garauna sakcha?
response_en: Yes, hormonal changes during pregnancy can cause mood swings.
response_ne: Ho, pregnancy ko dauraan hormonal changes le mood swings garauna sakcha.

question_en: Why do I feel tired during pregnancy?
question_ne: Pregnancy ko bela kina thakai lagcha?
response_en: Fatigue is common in early pregnancy due to hormonal changes and the body adjusting to pregnancy.
response_ne: Pregnancy ko suru ko dauraan hormonal changes ra body ko pregnancy ko adaptation le thakaunu common ho.

question_en: Can pregnancy affect my periods?
question_ne: Pregnancy le mero period affect garna sakcha?
response_en: Pregnancy causes periods to stop, but some women may experience spotting.
response_ne: Pregnancy ko dauraan periods stop hunchha, tara kunai mahila haruko spotting huna sakchha.

question_en: Can I be pregnant if I have a period?
question_ne: Ma period bhaye pani pregnant huna sakchhu?
response_en: It is very unlikely, but light bleeding may occur during pregnancy which can be mistaken for a period.
response_ne: Yo ekdamai sambhavit chaina, tara pregnancy ko dauraan light bleeding huna sakchha, jo period jasto lagna sakchha.

question_en: How can I confirm pregnancy?
question_ne: Pregnancy confirm kasari garne?
response_en: The best way to confirm pregnancy is to take a home pregnancy test and visit a doctor.
response_ne: Pregnancy confirm garne sabai bhanda ramro tarika bhaneko home pregnancy test garne ra doctor ko ma janey ho.

question_en: Can pregnancy cause nausea?
question_ne: Pregnancy le nausea cause garna sakcha?
response_en: Yes, nausea is a common early sign of pregnancy, especially in the first trimester.
response_ne: Ho, nausea pregnancy ko suru ko dauraan common sign ho, khas gari pahilo trimester ma.

question_en: Can my diet affect my chances of pregnancy?
question_ne: Mero diet le pregnancy garne chances ma asar garna sakcha?
response_en: Yes, a balanced diet can help improve fertility and your chances of getting pregnant.
response_ne: Ho, balanced diet le fertility improve garne ra pregnancy garna ko chances badhauna sakcha.

question_en: Can I get pregnant with irregular periods?
question_ne: Irregular periods bhayepachi ma pregnant huna sakchhu?
response_en: Yes, you can still get pregnant with irregular periods, but tracking ovulation can help increase your chances.
response_ne: Ho, irregular periods bhayepachi pani pregnant huna sakinchha, tara ovulation track garera chances badhauna sakinchha.

question_en: Can I take medicine during pregnancy?
question_ne: Pregnancy ko dauraan medicine lina milcha?
response_en: Always consult your doctor before taking any medication during pregnancy to ensure it's safe.
response_ne: Pregnancy ko dauraan medicine lina doctor sanga parikshan garera safe ho bhane matra lida thik huncha.

question_en: Can pregnancy make me feel more emotional?
question_ne: Pregnancy le malaai dherai emotional feel garauna sakcha?
response_en: Yes, pregnancy can cause increased emotional sensitivity due to hormonal changes.
response_ne: Ho, pregnancy le hormonal changes ko karan emotional sensitivity badhauna sakcha.

question_en: How can I tell if I'm pregnant early?
question_ne: Suru ma pregnant bhayeko ho bhane kasari thaha paune?
response_en: You may notice early pregnancy signs like a missed period, nausea, or changes in your breasts.
response_ne: Timile early pregnancy ko sanket haru jastai period miss garne, nausea, ya breasts ma changes dekhera thaha pauna sakchau.

question_en: What are the symptoms of early pregnancy?
question_ne: Suru ko pregnancy ko symptoms k ho?
response_en: Common symptoms include a missed period, nausea, fatigue, and changes in your breasts.
response_ne: Common symptoms haru period miss garne, nausea, fatigue, ra breasts ma changes hunchha.

question_en: Why do I feel nauseous when pregnant?
question_ne: Pregnancy ma malai kina nausea feel hunchha?
response_en: Nausea is caused by hormonal changes and is often referred to as morning sickness.
response_ne: Nausea hormonal changes le cause garne ho, ra yo aam torma morning sickness ko rupma janinchha.

question_en: Can pregnancy cause cramps?
question_ne: Pregnancy le cramps cause garna sakcha?
response_en: Yes, mild cramps can occur in early pregnancy due to changes in the uterus.
response_ne: Ho, mild cramps pregnancy ko suru ko dauraan uterus ma changes hunele huna sakchha.

question_en: What is a balanced diet?
question_ne: Balanced diet bhaneko k ho?
response_en: A balanced diet includes carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, and minerals in the right proportions for good health.
response_ne: Balanced diet bhaneko carbohydates, proteins, fats, minerals ra vitamins right amount  ma khanu ho

question_en: How many calories should I consume daily?
question_ne: Maile din ma kati calories khaanu parchha?
response_en: The number of calories you need depends on your age, gender, weight, height, and physical activity level.
response_ne: Dainik calories ko aawashyakta umer, linga, shaaririk gatibidhi, ra swasthya lakshya anusar farak hunchha, tara madhyama aayuko byakti ko laagi 2000-2500 calories huncha.

question_en: What foods help reduce period cramps?
question_ne: Mahina ko pet dukhai kam garna kun khana madad garna sakchha?
response_en: Foods rich in magnesium like bananas, spinach, and nuts can help reduce cramps.
response_ne: Magnesium le bhari khana jastai kera, palungo, ra badam le pet dukhai kam garna sakchha.

question_en: What should I eat during my period?
question_ne: Mahina ko bela ma maile ke khanuparcha?
response_en: Eat iron-rich foods like leafy greens, lentils, and lean meat to replenish lost iron.
response_ne: Hariyo patyaune sabji, masoor dal, ra lean meat jastai iron yukta khana khanuparcha.

question_en: What foods should I avoid during my period?
question_ne: Period ko bela k khana avoid garne?
response_en: Avoid salty, sugary, and processed foods, as they can cause bloating and mood swings.
response_ne: Nunilo, chiniyukta, ra processed khana parihej garnuparcha, kinaki yo phulne ra mood swing hunesamasyalai badhai dinchha.

question_en: Can spicy food affect my period?
question_ne: Period ko bela piro le asar parcha?
response_en: Spicy food may not directly affect your period but can cause digestive issues.
response_ne: Piro khana le sidha mahina ma prabhav nagaraune ho, tara pachan sambandhi samasya badhauna sakchha.

question_en: Does drinking coffee during periods cause problems?
question_ne: Mahina ko bela coffee piunda samasya hunchha?
response_en: Too much coffee can worsen cramps and cause dehydration, so it's best to limit it.
response_ne: Dherai coffee le pet dukhai badhaune ra sharir dehydrated garna sakchha, tyesai le coffee ko matra kam garnuparcha.

question_en: What foods can delay periods naturally?
question_ne: Kun khane kura le period dhilo huna sakcha?
response_en: Some believe consuming papaya, pineapple, or spicy food can affect period timing, but there is limited scientific evidence.
response_ne: Kehi manchele papaya, ananas, ra piro khana le mahina ko samay badalne ma biswas garne garchan, tara thulo vaigyanik pramaan chaina.

question_en: Can eating chocolate help during periods?
question_ne: Chocolate khada period ma help garcha?
response_en: Dark chocolate, which contains magnesium, may help reduce cramps and boost mood.
response_ne: Dark chocolate jasle magnesium linchha, le pet dukhai kam garna ra mood boost garna sakchha.

question_en: Is it okay to fast during my period?
question_ne: Mahina ko bela upwas basna thik ho?
response_en: Fasting can affect energy levels and cause weakness, so it's important to stay hydrated and eat nutrient-rich foods.
response_ne: Upwas le urja ko star kam garna ra kamjori huncha, tyesai le paani ramrari piune ra poshaktatv yukta khana khanu jaruri huncha.

question_en: Should I increase my water intake during my period?
question_ne: Mahina ko bela ma paani ko matra badhaune ho?
response_en: Yes, drinking more water helps reduce bloating and prevents dehydration.

question_ne: Period ko bela kati iron khanu parcha?
response_en: Women need 18 mg of iron daily, but this requirement increases during menstruation.
response_ne: Mahina ko bela ma mahila harulai dainik 18 mg bhandaa badhi iron ko aawashyakta huncha.

question_en: What foods are high in iron?
question_ne: Kun khane kura haru iron ma high hunchan?
response_en: Iron-rich foods include spinach, lentils, red meat, and tofu.
response_ne: Iron yukta khana ma palungo, masoor dal, rato masu, ra tofu sammilit hun.

question_ne: Mahina ko bela ma iron supplement linu huncha?
response_en: Yes, but consult a doctor if you have heavy bleeding or feel fatigued during your period.
response_ne: Ho, tara yadi dherei ragat bagcha ya thakai mahasoos huncha bhane doctor sanga paramarsha garnuparcha

question_en: Why do I crave sweets during my period?
question_ne: Mahina ko bela ma malai metho khana kina man lagchha?
response_en: Hormonal changes cause cravings for sweets, especially due to serotonin fluctuations
response_ne: Hormone ko badalaw le serotonin ko star farak parcha, jasko karan metho khana khane ichha badhauna sakchha.

question_en: What can I eat to reduce mood swings during my period?
question_ne: Mood swing kam garna maile ke khanuparcha?
response_en: Eating magnesium-rich foods like bananas and dark chocolate can stabilize mood.
response_ne: Kera ra dark chocolate jastai magnesium yukta khana le mood niyantran garna sakchha.

question_en: What diet is good for PCOS and PCOD?
question_ne: PCOS ra PCOD ko lagi kun aahaar ramro ho?
response_en: A low-sugar, high-fiber, and high-protein diet can help manage PCOS symptoms.
response_ne: Kam chini, dherai fiber ra protein yukta aahaar le PCOS ko lakshan niyantran garna sakchha.

question_en: What foods worsen PCOS symptoms?
question_ne: Kun khana le PCOS ko lakshan bigarna sakchha?
response_en: Processed foods, refined sugar, and trans fats can worsen PCOS symptoms.
response_ne: Processed khana, chini, ra trans fat le PCOS ko lakshan bigarna sakchha.

question_en: Can ginger tea help with period pain?
question_ne: Ginger tea le period pain kam garcha?
response_en: Yes, ginger tea has anti-inflammatory properties that relieve period cramps.
response_ne: Ho, ginger tea ma anti-inflammatory gun huncha, jasle mahina ko dukhai kam garchha.

question_en: Why do I crave junk food during my period?
question_ne: Mahina ko bela malai junk food khana kina man lagchha?
response_en: Hormonal changes increase serotonin demand, leading to cravings for comfort foods
response_ne: Hormone ko badalaw le serotonin ko aawashyakta badhaunchha, jasle comfort food khane ichha badhauna sakchha.

question_en: How can I control cravings during my period?
question_ne: Mahina ko bela craving kasari control garna sakchhu?
response_en: Eating healthy snacks like fruits, nuts, and whole grains can reduce cravings.
response_ne: Falful, badam, ra whole grains jastai healthy snacks khane le craving kam huncha.

question_en: Can diet improve PCOS symptoms?
question_ne: PCOS ko lakshan diet le improve garna sakchha?
response_en: Yes, a low-sugar, high-fiber, and anti-inflammatory diet can reduce PCOS symptoms.
response_ne: Ho, chini kam, dherai fiber, ra anti-inflammatory diet le PCOS ko lakshan kam garna sakchha.

question_en: Can dairy affect PCOS symptoms?
question_ne: Dairy le PCOS ko lakshan ma prabhav parcha?
response_en: Dairy may affect insulin levels in some people with PCOS, so consult a doctor.
response_ne: Kehi manche ko PCOS ma dairy le insulin ko star ma prabhav parcha, tyesai le doctor sanga paramarsha garnuparcha.

question_en: Should I avoid cold drinks during my period?
question_ne: Mahina ko bela ma thanda drink parihej garnu parcha?
response_en: Cold drinks don’t directly affect periods, but avoid sugary sodas to prevent bloating.
response_ne: Cold drink le sidha mahina ma prabhav nagaraune, tara sugary soda le phulne samasya badhauna sakchha.

question_en: Can I eat fast food during my period?
question_ne: Period ko bela fast food khana milcha?
response_en: It’s best to limit fast food as it contains high salt and unhealthy fats that cause bloating.
response_ne: Fast food ma dherai nun ra unhealthy fat huncha, jasle phulne samasya badhauna sakchha, tyesai le parihej garnuparcha.

question_en: How much water should I drink during my period?
question_ne: Period ko bela kati paani piunu parcha?
response_en: Drink 8-10 glasses of water daily to prevent bloating and dehydration.
response_ne: Dainik 8-10 glass paani piune le phulne ra dehydration bata bachna madad garchha.

question_en: Can I drink herbal tea during my period?
question_ne: Mahina ko bela herbal tea piuna sakchhu?
response_en: Yes, teas like chamomile and ginger tea help relieve cramps.
response_ne: Ho, chamomile ra ginger tea le dukhai kam garna madad garchha.

question_en: What is menopause?
question_ne: Menopause bhaneko ke ho?
response_en: Menopause is when periods stop permanently, usually after age 45-50.
response_ne: Menopause bhaneko period permanently rokinu ho, jastai 45-50 barsa pachhi.

question_en: When does menopause usually happen?
question_ne: Menopause kaile huncha?
response_en: Menopause typically occurs between ages 45 and 55, but it can happen earlier or later for some women.
response_ne: Menopause aam torma 45 dekhi 55 barsha ko bichma hunchha, tara kei mahilako lai yo arko samayma pani huna sakcha.

question_en: What are the symptoms of menopause?
question_ne: Menopause ko lakshyan k ho?
response_en: Symptoms of menopause include hot flashes, night sweats, mood changes, and irregular periods.
response_ne: Menopause ko lakshan haru ma hot flashes, raatko pasina, manobhaavna ma parivartan, ani aniyamit mahine hunchhan.

question_en: How can I manage hot flashes?
question_ne: Hot flashes kasari manage garne?
response_en: To manage hot flashes, try staying cool, wearing loose clothing, and avoiding spicy foods.
response_ne: Hot flashes samhalna, thanda rahne, dhilo kapda lagane, ani taikha khana bhagne garne.

question_en: Is menopause the same as perimenopause?
question_ne: Menopause ra perimenopause ek jasto ho?
response_en: No, menopause refers to the time when you haven't had a period for 12 months, while perimenopause is the transition period leading up to menopause.
response_ne: Hoina, menopause bhaneko tyo samay ho, jaba tapaile 12 mahina samma mahine na paunu hunchha, ra perimenopause bhaneko menopause samma ko badalne samay ho.

question_en: Can I still get pregnant after menopause?
question_ne: Menopause pachi ma garbhavati hunu sakchhu?
response_en: After menopause, pregnancy is very unlikely because ovulation stops, but if you are still having periods, pregnancy is possible.
response_ne: Menopause pachi, garbhavati hunu sambhavana nikai kam hunchha, kina bhane ovulation bandha hunchha, tara yadi tapaile mahine pairahi raheko cha bhane garbhavati hunu sambhavana cha.

question_en: What are night sweats?
question_ne: Raatko pasina ke ho?
response_en: Night sweats are episodes of sweating during sleep, commonly associated with menopause due to hormonal changes.
response_ne: Raatko pasina sutai raheko samaya ma pasina aune ho, jun menopause sanga sambandhit huncha hormonal parivartan ko karan.

question_en: What causes menopause?
question_ne: Menopause kasari huncha?
response_en: Menopause is caused by a decrease in the production of hormones such as estrogen and progesterone as a woman ages.
response_ne: Menopause mahilako umar barhne sath estrogen ra progesterone jasta hormone haru ko utpadan kam hune karan hunchha.

question_en: Can menopause affect my mood?
question_ne: Menopause le mood ma asar parcha?
response_en: : Yes, hormonal changes during menopause can cause mood swings, irritability, and anxiety.
response_ne: Ho, menopause ko samayma hormonal parivartan le manobhaavna haru, chidhchidhapan, ra chinta ko karan banaucha.

question_en: How long do menopause symptoms last?
question_ne: Menopause ko lakshan katha samay samma rahanchha?
response_en: Menopause symptoms can last for a few years, but they typically subside within 2-5 years after menopause begins.
response_ne: Menopause ko lakshan kehi barsa samma rahna sakcha, tara aam torma menopause suru bhayepachi 2-5 barsa bhitra chhato puri hunchhan.

question_en: What is perimenopause?
question_ne: Perimenopause ke ho?
response_en: Perimenopause is the transitional period before menopause, where hormonal changes begin to occur and menstrual cycles become irregular.
response_ne: Perimenopause menopause ko agadi ko samay ho, jaha hormonal parivartan haru suru huncha ra mahine aniyamit hunchha.

question_en: Can menopause cause weight gain?
question_ne: Menopause le wazan badhne ho?
response_en: Yes, hormonal changes during menopause can lead to weight gain, particularly around the abdomen.
response_ne: Ho, menopause ko samayma hormonal parivartan le wazan badhuna sakcha, khas garera petko chhetra ma.

question_en: Is it normal to miss periods during perimenopause?
question_ne: Perimenopause ko samayma mahine chhodi dine normal ho?
response_en: Yes, it is common to experience missed periods during perimenopause due to hormonal fluctuations.
response_ne: Ho, perimenopause ko samayma hormonal badlaav ko karan mahine chhodi dinu samanya ho.

question_en: Can menopause cause depression?
question_ne: Menopause le depression ko karan banaucha?
response_en: Yes, the hormonal changes during menopause can contribute to feelings of depression or sadness.
response_ne: Ho, menopause ko samayma hormonal badlaav le depression ya udasi ko bhawana haru ko karan banai sakcha.

question_en: Is it safe to take hormone replacement therapy?
question_ne: Hormone replacement therapy linu surakshit ho?
response_en: Hormone replacement therapy (HRT) can be safe for some women, but it depends on individual health conditions and should be discussed with a doctor.
response_ne: Hormone replacement therapy (HRT) kei mahilako lagi surakshit huna sakcha, tara yo vyakti ko swasthya awastha anusar farak parcha, ani doctor sanga charcha garna parcha.

question_en: What is vaginal dryness?
question_ne: Vaginal dryness bhaneko k ho?
response_en: Vaginal dryness is a common symptom of menopause, caused by a decrease in estrogen levels, making the vaginal tissues less moist.
response_ne: Vaginal dryness menopause ko ek samanya lakshan ho, jo estrogen ko star kam hune karan vaginal sookhne hunchha.

question_en: Can menopause cause hair loss?
question_ne: Menopause le kapal jharcha?
response_en: Yes, hormonal changes during menopause can lead to hair thinning or hair loss.
response_ne: Ho, menopause ko samayma hormonal parivartan le kapal patlo hune ya baal jharne garna sakcha.

question_en: Will my periods stop immediately after menopause?
question_ne: Menopause pachi mero mahine turunt bandahunchha?
response_en: No, periods gradually stop over time after menopause, with many women experiencing irregular cycles before they cease.
response_ne: Hoina, menopause pachi period bistarai bandh hunchhan, anek mahilale aniyamit cycle anubhav garne hunchhan bandh hunu agadi.

question_en: How can I exercise during my period?
question_ne: Period ko bela ma kasari exercise garna sakchu?
response_en: During your period, try lighter exercises like walking, yoga, or stretching. These can help relieve cramps and reduce fatigue. Intense exercises may be too tiring, so listen to your body.
response_ne: Tapaiko period ko samayama, haluka exercises jastai walking, yoga, ya stretching garna try garnu. Yo cramps kam garna ra thakan kam garna madat garna sakcha. Atyanta exercises dherai thakan dina sakcha, tesaile tapaiko sharir ko sunera garne.

question_en: What types of workouts are best during my menstrual phase?
question_ne: Kasto workout mahinawari ma ramro huncha?
response_en: Gentle activities like walking, swimming, or light yoga are great during menstruation. Focus on flexibility exercises and avoid high-impact activities
response_ne: Walking, swimming, ya haluka yoga jastai sabai gentle activities period ko samayama ramro hunchha. Flexibility exercises ma focus garnu ra high-impact activities avoid garnu.

question_en: How can I reduce menstrual cramps naturally?
question_ne: Ma prakitik tarikale menstrual cramps kasari kam garna sakchhu?
response_en: You can reduce cramps by applying heat, staying hydrated, exercising, and eating anti-inflammatory foods.
response_ne: Cramps kam garna tapai taato lagayera, pani khayera, exercise garera ra anti-inflammatory khana khayera saknuhunchha.

question_en: What are some foods to avoid during periods?
question_ne: Period ko belama kun khanaharu parhej garne?
response_en: Avoid processed foods, caffeine, and salty or sugary snacks.
response_ne: Processed foods, caffeine, ra dherai nimkha ra guliyo khana parhej garnuhos.

question_en: Is it normal to feel tired during periods?
question_ne: Periods ko belama thakais mannu normal ho?
response_en: Yes, hormonal changes can make you feel tired. Rest and hydration help.
response_ne: Ho, hormonal changes le timilai thakais jasto lagna sakcha. Rest ani pani khanu madad garcha.

question_en: Can exercise help with period pain?
