response_ne: Ho, genetics ya health problem le chhito menopause huna sakcha.

question_en: Why do I have painful periods?
question_ne: Mero period dukhne kina huncha?
response_en: Painful periods can be caused by endometriosis, fibroids, or hormonal imbalances.
response_ne: Endometriosis, fibroids, ya hormonal imbalance le dukhai huna sakcha.

question_en: Can an IUD affect my periods?
question_ne: IUD le mero period ma asar parcha?
response_en: Yes, some IUDs can make periods lighter, while others can cause heavier bleeding.
response_ne: Ho, kei IUD le period halka banauna sakcha, kei le dherai ragat flow garauna sakcha.

question_en: What are the signs of hormonal imbalance?
question_ne: Hormonal imbalance ko signs k ho?
response_en: Signs include irregular periods, weight gain, acne, and mood swings.
response_ne: Irregular period, weight gain, acne, ra mood swings hormonal imbalance ko sanket hun.

question_en: Can thyroid problems affect periods?
question_ne: Thyroid problem bhaye period irregular huncha?
response_en: Yes, both hypothyroidism and hyperthyroidism can disrupt the menstrual cycle.
response_ne: Ho, thyroid disorder le menstrual cycle bigarna sakcha.

question_en: What is adenomyosis?
question_ne: Adenomyosis vaneko k ho?
response_en: Adenomyosis occurs when the uterine lining grows into the muscle wall, causing pain and heavy periods.
response_ne: Adenomyosis bhane ko uterus ko lining muscle bhitra badhne condition ho, jasle dukhai ra dherai ragat flow huna sakcha.

question_en: Can UTIs cause vaginal itching?
question_ne: UTI le vaginal itching huna sakcha?
response_en: Yes, UTIs can cause irritation, burning, and itching around the vaginal area.
response_ne: Ho, UTI le irritation, burning, ra vaginal itching huna sakcha.

question_en: Why do I get blood clots during my period?
question_ne: Period ma ragat ka clots kina auncha?
response_en: Blood clots are normal, but very large clots may indicate a medical condition.
response_ne: Ragat ka thopa normal ho, tara dherai thulo bhaye health problem huna sakcha.

question_en: Can a cyst rupture during my period?
question_ne: Period ma ovarian cyst rupture huna sakcha?
response_en: Yes, cysts can rupture, causing sudden sharp pain.
response_ne: Ho, cyst rupture bhaye sudden pain huncha.

question_en: What is toxic shock syndrome (TSS)?
question_ne: Toxic shock syndrome vane ko k ho?
response_en: TSS is a rare but serious condition caused by bacterial toxins, often linked to tampon use.
response_ne: TSS ek dherai kam dekhine tara jatil condition ho, jun bacteria ko toxin le huncha ra tampon use sanga sambandhit huncha.

question_en: What should I do if I have heavy bleeding during my period?
question_ne: Yadi mero period ma dherai blood loss bhaye k garne?
response_en: If you experience heavy bleeding, use high-absorbency pads or tampons, and if the bleeding lasts more than 7 days or is very heavy, seek medical attention immediately.
response_ne: Yadi tapaiko dherai blood loss bhaye, long pad ya tampon haru prayog garnu hos, ra yadi blood loss 7 din samma rahi rahyo bhane turunta checkup garna jana parcha.

question_en: What should I do if I experience severe pelvic pain?
question_ne: ekdam pelvic pain bhaye k garne?
response_en: Severe pelvic pain could indicate a serious condition like an ovarian cyst rupture or ectopic pregnancy. It's crucial to seek immediate medical care.
response_ne: Gahiro pelvic dard le ovarian cyst ko phatnu ya ectopic pregnancy jaise gambhir awastha ko sign dina sakchha. Turunt doctor sanga checkup garnu mahatwapurna ho.

question_en: What are the signs of an ectopic pregnancy?
question_ne: Ectopic pregnancy ko signs ke ke ho?
response_en: Symptoms of an ectopic pregnancy may include severe abdominal pain, dizziness, fainting, and shoulder pain. If you suspect this, seek emergency medical help immediately.
response_ne: Ectopic pregnancy ko lakshan haru ma gahiro pet dard, chakkar aunu, behoshi, ra shoulder pain ho. Yadi yeh samasya cha bhane turunt emergency chikitsa garna jana parcha.

question_en: When should I go to the emergency room for cramps?
question_ne: Mero cramps ko lagi kahile emergency room jana parcha?
response_en: If your cramps are unusually intense, prolonged, or accompanied by symptoms like fever, vomiting, or fainting, it’s time to visit the emergency room.
response_ne: Yadi tapaiko cramps asadharan roopma gahiro, lamo samayako, ya jwar, ulti, ya behoshi jasta lakshan haru sanga ho bhane emergency room jana parcha.

question_en: What should I do if I feel faint during my period?
question_ne: Yadi maile mahawari ko samayama behoshi jasto mahsoos garchu bhane ke garne?
response_en: Fainting during your period could be due to low blood pressure or anemia. Lie down, drink water, and if it persists, seek medical attention.
response_ne: Mahawari ko samayama behoshi ko mahsoos low blood pressure ya anemia ko karan huna sakchha. Aram garera, paani piyo ra yadi yeh samasya rahencha bhane chikitsa garna jana parcha.

question_en: what to do if i haave period pain
question_ne: period ma ekdam bet dukhda k garne
response_en: When experiencing severe stomach pain, drink warm water, do light exercises, put hot water bag and take rest. If the pain is too much, consult a doctor!
response_ne: Pet dherai dukhda, garam pani piune,hot water bag rakhne, halka exercise garne, ra aaram garne. Dherai dukhai bhaye doctor sanga salah garnu!

question_en: i feel like i am about to fall?
question_ne: ma faint hola jasto bhairacha?
response_en: Fainting during your period could be due to low blood pressure or anemia. Lie down, drink water, and if it persists, seek medical attention.
response_ne: Mahawari ko samayama behoshi ko mahsoos low blood pressure ya anemia ko karan huna sakchha. Aram garera, paani piyo ra yadi yeh samasya rahencha bhane chikitsa garna jana parcha.

question_en: What is a miscarriage and when should I go to the doctor?
question_ne: Miscarriage ke ho ra maile doctor sanga kahile jana parcha?
response_en: A miscarriage is the loss of a pregnancy before 20 weeks. If you experience heavy bleeding, cramping, or discharge, see a doctor immediately.
response_ne: Miscarriage bhaneko pregnancy tarr rinu ho. Yadi tapaile bhari raktasrava, cramps, ya discharge anubhav garnu bhaye bhane turunt doctor sanga jana parcha.

question_en: How can I tell if I’m having an allergic reaction to a medication?
question_ne: Malai kunai medicine ko allergic reaction bhairako cha bhanera kasari tha paune?
response_en: If you experience symptoms like rash, difficulty breathing, or swelling, it may be an allergic reaction. Seek immediate medical help in case of severe symptoms.
response_ne: Yadi tapaile rashes, saans lene ma garo, ya soojan ko lakshan feel garnu bhaye bhane allergic reaction huna sakchha. Gahiro lakshan haru cha bhane turunta doctor lai dekhauna parcha.

question_en: What should I do if I experience sudden severe abdominal pain?
question_ne: Achanak ekdam pet dukhe k garne?
response_en: Sudden severe abdominal pain can be a sign of a ruptured cyst, infection, or other serious conditions. Get medical help immediately if the pain is sharp or doesn’t subside.
response_ne: Achanak gahiro pet dard ovarian cyst ko phatnu, sankraman, ya anya gambhir awastha ko signs ho sakchha. dherai pain bhane turunt doctor sanga kura garna jana parcha.

question_en: How do I know if I am at risk for a stroke during pregnancy?
question_ne: Pregnancy ma stroke ko danger cha bhane ke kasari pata laune?
response_en: Symptoms of a stroke during pregnancy include sudden weakness, numbness, trouble speaking, and severe headache. Seek immediate emergency care if these occur.
response_ne: Garbhavasthama stroke ko lakshan haru ma achanak kamjori, sujan, kura garna garoee, ra gahiro matha dard sammilit chhan. Yadi yeh lakshan haru dekhna bhaye bhane turunt emergency care garna parcha.

question_en: What should I do if I suspect a blood clot?
question_ne: Yadi maile rakt ko clot bhayeko ho bhane ke garne?
response_en: If you suspect a blood clot, you may experience swelling, redness, or warmth in one leg. Seek medical help immediately, as blood clots can be life-threatening.
response_ne: Yadi tapaile rakt ko clot bhayeko mahsoos garnu bhaye, tapaile ek jhode sujan, lalima, ya garmi anubhav garna sakchha. Turunt chikitsa garna jana parcha, kina ki rakt ko clot jeevan hani sakchha.

question_en: What are the signs of gestational diabetes?
question_ne: Gestational diabetes ko lakshan ke ke ho?
response_en: Signs of gestational diabetes include excessive thirst, frequent urination, fatigue, and blurred vision. If you experience these, consult your doctor for screening.
response_ne: Gestational diabetes ko lakshan haru ma atyadhik pyaas, barambar peshab, thakaune, ra dhundhalo dekhne sammilit chhan. Yadi yeh sabai ho bhane doctor sanga check garne.

question_en: What causes preeclampsia during pregnancy?
question_ne: Garbhavasthama preeclampsia ko kaaran ke ho?
response_en: Preeclampsia is caused by high blood pressure and organ damage during pregnancy. It can lead to serious complications if left untreated. Seek medical attention if symptoms occur.
response_ne: Preeclampsia high blood pressure ra organ ko nukhsan ko karan bhayeko ho garbhavasthama. Yadi lakshan haru dekhincha bhane turunt chikitsa garna jana parcha.

question_en: How do I know if I have a miscarriage?
question_ne: Maile miscarriage bhayeko ho bhane ke kasari pata garne?
response_en: Symptoms of a miscarriage include heavy bleeding, cramping, and passing tissue. If you experience these, consult your doctor immediately.
response_ne: Miscarriage ko lakshan haru bhari raktasrava, cramps, ra tissue nikaliye ko dekhne ho. Yadi yeh sabai ho bhane doctor sanga turunt jana parcha.

question_en: What is an ectopic pregnancy and how does it affect the body?
question_ne: Ectopic pregnancy ke ho ra yo shareerma kasto asar puryaunchha?
response_en: An ectopic pregnancy occurs when the fertilized egg implants outside the uterus, typically in a fallopian tube. This can lead to severe complications and requires immediate treatment.
response_ne: Ectopic pregnancy tabhi hunchha jab fertilized egg uterus ko bahira, praya fallopian tube ma implant hunchha. Yo gambhir samasya ho ra turunt upachar garna parcha.

question_en: What should I do if I have high blood pressure during pregnancy?
question_ne: Garbhavasthama high blood pressure cha bhane ke garne?
response_en: If you have high blood pressure during pregnancy, your doctor may monitor you regularly and recommend lifestyle changes or medications. Seek medical attention immediately if symptoms worsen.
response_ne: Garbhavasthama high blood pressure bhaye bhane doctor le tapaiko monitoring garne ra lifestyle changes ya dawa diye sakchha. Yadi lakshan haru garo hunchha bhane turunt doctor sanga jana parcha.

question_en: How can I tell if I have a urinary tract infection (UTI) during pregnancy?
question_ne: Garbhavasthama urinary tract infection (UTI) bhayeko ho bhane ke kasari pata garne?
response_en: Symptoms of a UTI include painful urination, cloudy urine, and a frequent urge to urinate. Consult your doctor if you suspect a UTI during pregnancy.
response_ne: UTI ko lakshan haru ma peshab garna dard, mandaa peshab, ra barambar peshab janma sammilit chhan. Garbhavasthama UTI bhayeko cha bhane doctor sanga check garne.

question_en: What should I do if I experience swelling during pregnancy?
question_ne: Garbhavasthama sujan bhayeko ho bhane ke garne?
response_en: Swelling is common during pregnancy, but excessive swelling, especially in the hands, face, and legs, could indicate a complication like preeclampsia. Seek medical attention.
response_ne: Garbhavasthama sujan sadharan ho, tara atyadhik sujan, haath, mukha, ra jhode ma, preeclampsia jasta gambhir samasya ko chinhn ho sakchha. Turunt doctor sanga jana parcha.

question_en: What are the risks of a premature birth?
question_ne: Premature birth ko khatra ke ke ho?
response_en: Premature birth occurs before 37 weeks of pregnancy and may lead to complications such as respiratory issues, underdeveloped organs, and developmental delays.
response_ne: Premature birth 37 hapta ko agi hunchha ra yo respiratory samasya, purna vikas bhayeko ang, ra vikasma deri haru ko karan huna sakchha.

question_en: How can I prevent pregnancy complications?
question_ne: Garbhavasthama complications ko rokhtham ke garne?
response_en: To reduce the risk of complications, maintain a healthy diet, avoid smoking and alcohol, attend regular prenatal checkups, and follow your doctor's advice.
response_ne: Garbhavasthama complications ko risk kam garna, swasthya aahar, smoking ra alcohol ko parhej, niyamit prenatal checkups garne, ra doctor ko sala anusaran garne.

question_en: What should I do if I have constant abdominal pain during pregnancy?
question_ne: Garbhavasthama lagatar pet ko dard bhayeko ho bhane ke garne?
response_en: Constant abdominal pain during pregnancy could be a sign of complications like miscarriage, ectopic pregnancy, or placenta issues. Seek medical help immediately.
response_ne: Garbhavasthama lagatar pet ko dard garbhavasthama miscarriage, ectopic pregnancy, ya placenta ko samasya ko chinhn ho sakchha. Turunt doctor sanga jana parcha.

question_en: What is placental abruption and how does it affect pregnancy?
question_ne: Placental abruption ke ho ra yo garbhavastha ma kasari prabhavit garne?
response_en: Placental abruption occurs when the placenta detaches from the uterine wall before delivery. It can lead to bleeding, preterm birth, or fetal distress. Immediate medical help is required.
response_ne: Placental abruption tabhi hunchha jab placenta uterus ko diwaro bata delivery ko agadi detach hunchha. Yo raktasrava, premature birth, ya fetal distress ko karan huna sakchha. Turunt chikitsa garna parcha.

question_en: What are the signs of a stroke during pregnancy?
question_ne: Garbhavasthama stroke ko lakshan ke ke ho?
response_en: Signs of a stroke include sudden numbness, difficulty speaking, severe headache, blurred vision, and dizziness. If these occur, seek emergency medical attention immediately.
response_ne: Stroke ko lakshan haru ma achanak sujan, kura garna garoee, gahiro matha dard, dhundhalo dekhne, ra chakkar aunu sammilit chha. Yadi yeh dekhincha bhane turunt emergency chikitsa garne.

question_en: What is a blood clot during pregnancy and how can it be dangerous?
question_ne: Garbhavasthama rakt ko clot ke ho ra yo kasari khatarnaak hunchha?
response_en: Blood clots during pregnancy can form in the legs (deep vein thrombosis) or lungs (pulmonary embolism). They can lead to life-threatening complications like stroke or organ failure.
response_ne: Garbhavasthama rakt ko clot jhode ma (deep vein thrombosis) ya fefda ma (pulmonary embolism) banne sakchha. Yo stroke ya ang ko akalpan jaise gambhir samasya ko karan huna sakchha.

question_en: How do I know if I have a molar pregnancy?
question_ne: Molar pregnancy bhayeko ho bhane ke kasari pata garne?
response_en: Molar pregnancy occurs when abnormal tissue grows instead of a fetus. It may cause symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and abnormal bleeding. Seek medical help immediately.
response_ne: Molar pregnancy tabhi hunchha jab asamanya tissue garbhako sthana ma vikas hunchha. Yo nausea, ulti, ra asadharan raktasrava jaise lakshan haru ko karan huna sakchha. Turunt chikitsa garna parcha.

question_en: What should I do if I experience shortness of breath during pregnancy?
question_ne: Garbhavasthama saans lene ma garoee bhayeko ho bhane ke garne?
response_en: Shortness of breath during pregnancy may indicate issues like blood clots, heart problems, or anemia. Seek immediate medical attention if this occurs.
response_ne: Garbhavasthama saans lene ma garoee raktako clot, hridaya sambandhi samasya, ya anemia ko karan huna sakchha. Yadi yeh dekhincha bhane turunt chikitsa garna parcha.

question_en: What is hyperemesis gravidarum and how does it affect pregnancy?
question_ne: Hyperemesis gravidarum ke ho ra yo garbhavastha ma kasari prabhavit garne?
response_en: Hyperemesis gravidarum is severe nausea and vomiting during pregnancy that leads to dehydration and weight loss. It requires medical attention and may require hospitalization.
response_ne: Hyperemesis gravidarum garbhavasthama gahiro nausea ra ulti ho jasle dehydration ra wajan ghatne ho. Yo chikitsa garna parcha ra kabhi kabhi hospitalization ko awashyakta hunchha.

question_en: What are the risks of preterm labor?
question_ne: Preterm labor ko khatra ke ke ho?
response_en: Preterm labor occurs before 37 weeks of pregnancy and may cause complications for the baby, such as breathing problems or underdeveloped organs. Immediate medical care is needed.
response_ne: Preterm labor 37 hapta bhanda agi garbhavastha ko suru hunchha ra yo baby ko liye sambhavit samasya haru jaise saans lene ko samasya ya underdeveloped ang haru ko karan huna sakchha. Turunt chikitsa garna parcha.

question_en: What is a uterine rupture and how does it affect pregnancy?
question_ne: Uterine rupture ke ho ra yo garbhavastha ma kasari prabhavit garne?
response_en: Uterine rupture is a tear in the uterus that can lead to severe bleeding, fetal distress, and a need for emergency surgery. Immediate medical help is critical.
response_ne: Uterine rupture uterus ko chhena ho jasle bhari raktasrava, fetal distress, ra turunt surgery ko awashyakta garna sakchha. Turunt chikitsa garna parcha.

question_en: What should I do if I experience sudden swelling of my face or hands during pregnancy?
question_ne: pregnancy me achanak hath ya mukh swell bhaye k garne?
response_en: Sudden swelling in the face or hands during pregnancy may indicate preeclampsia. Seek immediate medical attention as it can be life-threatening.
response_ne: Pregnancy ma achanak mukh ya haath ma swell preeclampsia ko chinhn ho sakchha. Yo jeevan khatarnak huna sakchha, ra turunt chikitsa garna jana parcha.

question_en: How can I tell if I have a urinary tract infection during pregnancy?
question_ne: Garbhavasthama urinary tract infection bhayeko ho bhane ke kasari pata garne?
response_en: Symptoms of a UTI during pregnancy include pain during urination, cloudy or foul-smelling urine, and pelvic discomfort. Consult your doctor if you experience these.
response_ne: Garbhavasthama UTI ko lakshan haru ma peshab garna dard, ganaune peshab, ra pelvic discomfort sammilit chhan. Yadi tapaile yeh sabai mahsoos garne ho bhane doctor sanga jana parcha.

question_en: What are the common symptoms of pregnancy?
question_ne: Pregnancy ko symptoms k ho?
response_en: Common symptoms of pregnancy include missed periods, nausea, vomiting, fatigue, tender breasts, and frequent urination.
response_ne: Pregnancy ko common symptoms haru ma period miss hune, nausea, ulti, thakaune, kaanpne breasts, ra barambar peshab janma sammilit chha.

question_en: what to do if i am having severe stomach pain?
question_ne: ekdam pet dukhda k garne?
response_en: When experiencing severe stomach pain, drink warm water, do light exercises, and take rest. If the pain is too much, consult a doctor!
response_ne: Pet dherai dukhda, garam pani piune, halka exercise garne, ra aaram garne. Dherai dukhai bhaye doctor sanga salah garnu!

question_en: How do I know if I have ovulation pain?
question_ne: Malai ovulation ko pain bhako ho bhane ke kasari thaha paune?
response_en: Ovulation pain typically occurs around the middle of your cycle, often on one side of the abdomen. It is sharp and brief, and may be accompanied by slight spotting.
response_ne: Ovulation ko pain usually aaphno cycle ko bich hunchha, jastai ek thauma gahiro pain hune. Yo sharp ra chhoto hunchha ra thorai spotting pani huna sakchha.

question_en: What are the signs of an irregular period?
question_ne: Irregular period ko signs k k ho?
response_en: Signs of an irregular period may include variations in the cycle length, missed periods, or periods that are heavier or lighter than normal. Consult your doctor if this occurs.
response_ne: Irregular period ko lakshan haru cycle ko length ma parivartan, period miss hune, ya normal bhanda bhari ya halka hune chha. Yadi yo dekhincha bhane doctor sanga check garne.

question_en: What does it mean if I am feeling dizzy and lightheaded?
question_ne: malai chakkar aairako cha bhanya k ho?
response_en: Feeling dizzy and lightheaded can be caused by low blood pressure, dehydration, anemia, or other conditions. If persistent, consult a healthcare provider.
response_ne: Chakkar aune jasto garo hune reasons low blood pressure, dehydration, anemia, ya anya samasya huna sakchha. Yadi yo dherai time samma raheko cha bhane checkup garne.

question_en: What should I do if I have a fever during pregnancy?
question_ne: prega ma jworo aaye ke garne?
response_en: A fever during pregnancy can indicate an infection. It's important to consult your doctor immediately to determine the cause and appropriate treatment.
response_ne: Garbhavasthama jwar ko lakshan infection ko karan huna sakchha. Doctor sanga turunt check garne ra karan ra upachar ko barema thaha paune.

question_en: What does it mean if I have unusual discharge?
question_ne: mero ajeeb discharge bhairako cha?
response_en: Unusual discharge may indicate an infection or hormonal imbalance. It could be yellow, green, or have an unpleasant odor. See a doctor for a proper diagnosis.
response_ne: Asadharan discharge infection ya hormonal imbalance ko signs ho sakchha. Yo pahelo, hariyo, wa ekdam ganaune huna sakcha. Doctor sanga check garne.

question_en: What does it mean if I have unusual discharge?
question_ne: mero arkai discharge bhairako cha?
response_en: Unusual discharge may indicate an infection or hormonal imbalance. It could be yellow, green, or have an unpleasant odor. See a doctor for a proper diagnosis.
response_ne: Asadharan discharge infection ya hormonal imbalance ko signs ho sakchha. Yo pahelo, hariyo, wa ekdam ganaune huna sakcha. Doctor sanga check garne.

question_en: What are the signs of anemia during pregnancy?
question_ne: pregancy ma anemia ko signs k ho?
response_en: Signs of anemia include fatigue, weakness, pale skin, shortness of breath, dizziness, and headaches. If these symptoms occur, consult your doctor.
response_ne: Anemia ko lakshan haru thakaune, kamjori, pale hune, saans lene garo hune, chakkar aaune jasto ho. Yadi yeh sabai dekhincha bhane doctor sanga check garne.

question_en: How do I know if I am dehydrated during pregnancy?
question_ne: Garbhavasthama dehydration bhayeko ho bhane ke kasari thaha paune
response_en: Symptoms of dehydration include dark urine, dry mouth, dizziness, and fatigue. Drink plenty of water and consult your doctor if symptoms persist.
response_ne: Dehydration ko lakshan haru ma kalo peshab, suko mukh, chakkar, ra thakaune sammilit chhan. Dherai paani pyaunuhos ra yadi lakshan rahichha bhane doctor sanga check garne.

question_en: What should I do if I am feeling nauseous during pregnancy?
question_ne: Garbhavasthama nausea bhayeko ho bhane ke garne?
response_en: Nausea during pregnancy is common, especially in the first trimester. Eat small, frequent meals and avoid strong odors. Consult your doctor if it persists or worsens.
response_ne: Garbhavasthama nausea pahilo trimester ma sadharan ho. Chhoto, barambar khana khanu ra strong smell bata tadha basnu. Yadi yeh rahichha bhane doctor sanga check garne.

question_en: What are the different types of contraceptive methods?
question_ne: Contraceptive ko types k k ho?
response_en: Contraceptive methods include hormonal methods (e.g., birth control pills, patches, injections), barrier methods (e.g., condoms), and natural methods (e.g., tracking fertility).
response_ne: Contraceptive ko tareekaharu hormonal methods (jasto birth control pills, patches, injections), barrier methods (jasto condoms), ra natural methods (jasto fertility track garne) sammilit chha.

question_en: How effective is birth control?
question_ne: Birth control kati effect hunchha?
response_en: Birth control is highly effective when used correctly. For example, birth control pills are about 99% effective, while condoms are about 85% effective.
response_ne: Birth control correct way bata use gare useful hunchha. Jastai birth control pills lagbhag 99% effective hunchha, ra condoms lagbhag 85% effective hunchha.

question_en: Can I use birth control if I have irregular periods?
question_ne: Yadi malai irregular periods hunchha bhane ke birth control useful hunchha?
response_en: Yes, birth control can help regulate menstrual cycles in people with irregular periods. It can also reduce heavy bleeding and manage other symptoms.
response_ne: Ho, birth control irregular periods bhayeka manche ma menstrual cycles ko niyantran garna madat garna sakchha. Yo bhari blood flow ra anya lakshan haru lai control garna sakchha.

question_en: What are the side effects of using birth control pills?
question_ne: Birth control pills ko side effects ke ke ho?
response_en: Side effects of birth control pills may include nausea, headaches, mood changes, weight gain, and spotting between periods. Consult your doctor if you experience these.
response_ne: Birth control pills ko side effects ma nausea, tauko dukhne, manasik parivartan, weight badhna, ra period ko bichma spotting sammilit chha. Yadi yeh dekhincha bhane doctor sanga check garne.

question_en: How do condoms work as contraception?
question_ne: Condoms kasari kaam garcha?
response_en: Condoms are a barrier method of contraception that prevents sperm from reaching the egg. They are also the only method that protects against sexually transmitted infections (STIs).
response_ne: Condoms contraception ko barrier method ho jasle sperm lai female body chirna bata rokcha. Yo sirf ek matra method ho jasle sexually transmitted infections (STIs) bata bachcha.

question_en: Can I use contraceptives if I am breastfeeding?
question_ne: Yadi ma breastfeeding garna sakchhu bhane ke contraceptives upayogi hunchha?
response_en: Yes, there are certain types of contraceptives that are safe to use while breastfeeding, such as progestin-only pills and IUDs. Consult your doctor to find the best option.
response_ne: Ho, kehi prakar ko contraceptives breastfeeding ko samay upayogi chhan, jastai progestin-only pills ra IUDs. Afno doctor sanga check garne ra best option chhanna saknu hunchha.

question_en: What is an IUD and how does it work?
question_ne: IUD ke ho ra yo kasari kaam garchha?
response_en: An IUD (intrauterine device) is a small T-shaped plastic and copper device placed inside the uterus. It prevents sperm from fertilizing the egg and can be effective for 3-10 years.
response_ne: IUD (intrauterine device) ek chhoto T-shape ko plastic ra copper ko device ho jaslai uterus bhitra rakhincha. Yo sperm lai fuse huna bata bachauchha ra 3-10 barsha samma prabhavi hunchha.

question_en: Can contraceptives help with acne?
question_ne: Contraceptives le acne ma madat garna sakchha?
response_en: Some hormonal contraceptives, such as birth control pills, can help reduce acne by regulating hormones. Consult your doctor to see if it’s right for you.
response_ne: Kehi hormonal contraceptives, jaslai birth control pills bhanincha, acne ko niyantran garna madat garna sakchha, hormone haru lai control garera. Apno doctor sanga salaha garne.

question_en: Are there natural contraceptive methods?
question_ne: k natural contraceptive methods chhan?
response_en: Natural contraceptive methods include fertility awareness, withdrawal method, and breastfeeding. However, these methods are less effective than other forms of contraception.
response_ne: Natural contraceptive methods fertility awareness, withdrawal method, ra breastfeeding sammilit chhan. Tara, yeh methods anya contraceptive methods bhanda kam prabhavi hunchha.

question_en: What is the difference between the pill and the patch?
question_ne: Pill ra patch ko farak ke ho?
response_en: The pill is an oral contraceptive taken daily, while the patch is a small adhesive worn on the skin for a week at a time. Both methods release hormones to prevent pregnancy.
response_ne: Pill ek oral contraceptive ho jaslai daily basis ma khana parcha, ra patch ek chhoto chhipko adhesive ho jaslai skin ma ek hapta samma rakheko hunchha. duitai method haru le pregnancy rokna hormones dincha.

question_en: How do emergency contraceptive pills work?
question_ne: Emergency contraceptive pills kasari kaam garchha?
response_en: Emergency contraceptive pills, such as the "morning-after pill," work by delaying or inhibiting ovulation or fertilization. They are most effective within 72 hours after unprotected sex.
response_ne: Emergency contraceptive pills, jaslai "morning-after pill" pani bhannchha, ovulation ya fertilization ko samay pachi rokne ya dhila garne kaam garchha. Yo 72 ghanta bhitra khaye effective huncha.

question_en: Can I get pregnant if I miss one birth control pill?
question_ne: Yadi ma ek birth control pill miss garchu bhane ke ma pregnant huna sakchhu?
response_en: Missing one pill can increase your chances of pregnancy, especially if it is not taken within 24 hours. Follow the instructions for missed pills to maintain effectiveness.
response_ne: Ek pill miss gare pregnancy ko chance badhauna sakcha, yadi 24 ghanta bhitra lena garne ho bhane.

question_en: How long can I use a contraceptive implant?
question_ne: Contraceptive implant kati samaya samma prayog garna sakchhu?
response_en: A contraceptive implant can be used for 3 to 5 years, depending on the type. It is a small rod inserted under the skin, releasing hormones to prevent pregnancy.
response_ne: Contraceptive implant 3 dekhi 5 barsha samma prayog garna sakchha, tyasko prakar anusar. Yo ek chhoto rod ho jasle hormones chhodeko garne.

question_en: Are there contraceptives for men?
question_ne: kta haru ko lagi contraceptives chha?
response_en: Yes, there are contraceptive options for men, such as condoms and vasectomy. These methods prevent sperm from reaching the egg.
response_ne: Ho, purush haru ko lagi contraceptive options chha, jastai condoms ra vasectomy.

question_en: Can I use contraceptives if I am over 40?
question_ne: if ma 40 barsa bhanda badi chhu bhane ke contraceptives effective hunchha?
response_en: Yes, many contraceptive options, including birth control pills and IUDs, are safe for people over 40. However, it’s important to consult your doctor for personalized advice.
response_ne: Ho, dherai contraceptive options, birth control pills ra IUDs samet, 40 barsa bhanda badi manisama pani upayogi chhan. Tara, apno doctor sanga salaha garne zaruri chha.

question_en: What is a hormonal IUD?
question_ne: Hormonal IUD ke ho?
response_en: A hormonal IUD is a small, T-shaped device placed inside the uterus that releases hormones to prevent pregnancy. It can last for 3 to 5 years depending on the type.
response_ne: Hormonal IUD ek chhoto, T-shape ko device ho jaslai uterus bhitra rakheko chha ra hormones chhodeko garne, yo 3 dekhi 5 barsha samma chalcha.

question_en: Can contraceptives affect my mood or mental health?
question_ne: Contraceptives le mero manasik swasthya ya mood ma prabhav parcha?
response_en: Some contraceptives, especially hormonal ones, may affect mood or mental health. If you experience significant changes, consult your doctor for alternative options.
response_ne: Kehi contraceptives, vises garera hormonal ones, mood ya manasik swasthya ma prabhav parna sakchha. Yadi thulo parivartan dekhincha bhane, doctor sanga salaha garne.

question_en: Can I use birth control if I have a history of blood clots?
question_ne: Yadi malaai blood clots ko itihas chha bhane ke ma birth control use garna sakchhu?
response_en: Some birth control methods, particularly those with estrogen, may increase the risk of blood clots. It's important to discuss options with your doctor if you have a history.
response_ne: Kehi birth control methods, bisesa estrogen waala, blood clots ko risk badhaune sakchha. Yadi apno itihas cha bhane doctor sanga options ko barema salaha garne.

question_en: What is the best contraceptive method for me?
question_ne: Mero lagi sabai bhanda ramro contraceptive method ke ho?
response_en: The best contraceptive method varies depending on factors such as health, convenience, and personal preference. Consult your healthcare provider to find the right choice.
response_ne: Ramro contraceptive method swasthya, suvidha, ra vyaktigat wish ko adhar ma farak hunchha. Apno doctor sanga salaha garne.

question_en: What is a copper IUD and how does it work?
question_ne: Copper IUD ke ho ra yo kasari kaam garchha?
response_en: A copper IUD is a small T-shaped device inserted into the uterus. It releases copper to prevent sperm from fertilizing the egg and can last up to 10 years.
response_ne: Copper IUD ek chhoto T-shape ko device ho jaslai uterus bhitra rakhne garincha. Yo copper chhodeko garne jasle sperm lai andaja bata bachchha ra 10 barsha samma chalcha.

question_en: should i not go to temples during period?
question_ne: hamilai mandir jana thik huncha ki hunna?
response_en: This is a cultural stigma, not a medical rule. There is no health reason preventing women from visiting temples during their period. It’s important to challenge such taboos.
response_ne: Yo ek sanskritik stigma ho, medical niyam hoina. Period ko samay ma mahilale mandir jaanu parne ko lagi kunai swasthya samasya chaina. Yo taboos lai challenge garnu zaruri ho.

question_en: Is it true that women can't visit temples during their period?
question_ne: kt haru period bhayepachhi mandir jaanu hudaina?

response_ne: Yo ek sanskritik stigma ho, medical niyam hoina. Period ko samay ma mahilale mandir jaanu parne ko lagi kunai swasthya samasya chaina. Yo taboos lai challenge garnu zaruri ho.

question_ne: Period ko samay mahilale dharmik sthal haru ma jana sakdainan, kina?
response_en: Many religious traditions consider menstruation impure, but these beliefs are based on cultural practices rather than science. Menstruating women are perfectly healthy.
response_ne: Dherai dharmik parampara haru menstruation lai impure manchhan, tara yo dharmik anushasan haru ko adhar ma ho, science ko adhar ma hoina. Period bhayeka mahilale puri tarikale swasthya cha.

question_en: Can women touch holy books during their period?
question_ne: Period ko samay mahila le pavitra kitab haru chhune garna sakchhan?
response_en: There is no scientific or religious basis for banning women from touching holy books during their period. This is a cultural belief, not a religious requirement.
response_ne: Pavitra kitab haru chhune ko lagi period ko samay mahilale manahi garna sakdainan bhanne kunai scientific ya dharmik aadhar chhaina. Yo ek sanskritik vichar ho.

question_en: Why are women considered impure during menstruation?
question_ne: Menstruation ko samay mahila le impure kina manincha?
response_en: The belief that women are impure during menstruation is based on outdated cultural ideas. Menstruation is a natural, healthy process that does not make women impure.
response_ne: Menstruation ko samay mahilale impure manne vichar purano sanskritik dharna ko adhar ma ho. Menstruation ek prakritik ra swasthya prakriya ho, jasle mahilale impure banaundaina.

response_en: This is another example of a cultural taboo, with no health justification. Women can fully participate in family celebrations regardless of menstruation.

question_en: Why do some people say women can't touch plants during menstruation?
question_ne: Kehi manis haru bhancha ki mahilale period ko samay ropaharu chhuna sakdainan?
response_en: This is an outdated belief with no scientific backing. There is no reason why women can’t touch plants or do gardening during menstruation.
response_ne: Yo ek purano vichar ho, jasko kunai scientific adhar chhaina. Period ko samay mahilale ropaharu chhuna sakdainan bhanne kunai karan chhaina.

response_en: Isolation during menstruation is rooted in old cultural practices that associate menstruation with impurity. It’s essential to break these traditions to empower women.
response_ne: Period ko samay mahilale alag rakhne parampara purano sanskritik abhipray ko adhar ma ho, jasle menstruation lai impure manchha. Yo parampara haru todne zaruri ho.

response_en: Yes, a woman can pray during her period. There is no religious or health reason preventing her from doing so. This is a societal belief rather than a true restriction.
response_ne: Ho, mahilale period ko samay prarthana garna sakchha. Yo garne ma kunai dharmik ya swasthya samasya chaina. Yo ek samajik vichar ho, jo sahi chhaina.

question_en: Can we go to temple during period?
question_ne: Period bela hami mandir jana milcha?
response_en: Yes, a woman can pray during her period. There is no religious or health reason preventing her from doing so. This is a societal belief rather than a true restriction.
response_ne: Ho, mahilale period ko samay prarthana garna sakchha. Yo garne ma kunai dharmik ya swasthya samasya chaina. Yo ek samajik vichar ho, jo sahi chhaina.

question_en: Is it unhealthy for a woman to participate in sports during menstruation?
question_ne: Period ko samay mahilale sports ma bhag lina garo chha ki unhealthy ho?
response_en: No, it is not unhealthy for a woman to participate in sports during menstruation. In fact, exercise can help alleviate menstrual discomfort and improve mood.
response_ne: Period ko samay mahilale sports ma bhag lina garo chhaina. Asalma, exercise le menstruation ko asuvidha haru kam garna ra manasik swasthya sudharna madat garne chha.

question_en: What foods to have in period?
question_ne: Period ma k khanu ramro ho?
response_en: Foods rich in magnesium like bananas, spinach, and nuts can help reduce cramps.
response_ne: Magnesium le bhari khana jastai kera, palungo, ra badam le pet dukhai kam garna sakchha.

question_en: What to eat in a period?
question_ne: Period ma k khanu thik ho?
response_en: Foods rich in magnesium like bananas, spinach, and nuts can help reduce cramps.
response_ne: Magnesium le bhari khana jastai kera, palungo, ra badam le pet dukhai kam garna sakchha.

question_en: Why do I have brown discharge before my period?
question_ne: Period agadi mero kina brown color discharge aaucha?
response_en: Brown discharge typically means that old blood is leaving the uterus, often occurring at the beginning or end of a period.
response_ne: "Brown discharge matlab pahila ko blood hajur ko uterus bata flow bhaira ko cha. yo dherai jaso period ko start wa end tira huncha"

question_en: What is progesterone?
question_ne: Progesterone ke ho?
response_en: Progesterone is a hormone produced mainly in the ovaries after ovulation. It plays a crucial role in regulating the menstrual cycle and maintaining the early stages of pregnancy.
response_ne: Progesterone ek hormone ho, jo ovulation pachi ovaries ma utpadan huncha. Yo menstrual cycle lai niyantran garna ra garbhavastha ko suru ko charan ma sahayak huncha.

question_en: What is serotonin?
question_ne: Serotonin ke ho?
response_en: Serotonin is a neurotransmitter that contributes to feelings of well-being and happiness. It also plays a role in regulating mood, sleep, and digestion.
response_ne: Serotonin ek neurotransmitter ho, jo sukha ra khushi ko anubhav garna madat garcha. Yo mood, nidra, ra pachen lai niyantran garna pani bhumika khelda cha.

question_en: What is estrogen?
question_ne: Estrogen ke ho?
response_en: Estrogen is a primary female sex hormone responsible for the development and regulation of the female reproductive system and secondary sexual characteristics.
response_ne: Estrogen mahila ko mukhya sex hormone ho, jo mahila ko reproductive system ra dosro lingik visheshta ko bikas ra niyantran ko lagi jimmewar huncha.

question_en: What is Rh factor?
question_ne: Rh factor ke ho?
response_en: The Rh factor is a protein found on the surface of red blood cells. If your blood has this protein, you're Rh positive; if not, you're Rh negative.
response_ne: Rh factor ek protein ho, jo red blood cell ko satah ma paaincha. Yadi tapai ko raktama yo protein cha bhane, tapai Rh sakaratmak hunuhuncha; yadi chaina bhane, Rh nakaratmak hunuhuncha.

question_en: How does Rh factor affect pregnancy?
question_ne: Rh factor le garbhavastha ma kasari asar garcha?
response_en: If an Rh-negative mother is carrying an Rh-positive baby, her body might produce antibodies against the baby's blood cells, potentially leading to complications. This condition is known as Rh incompatibility.
response_ne: Yadi ek Rh nakaratmak aama le Rh sakaratmak shishu lai garbhma rakheki chan bhane, unko sharir le shishu ko raktakoshika viruddha antibody banauna sakcha, jasle garbhavastha ma samasya utpanna garna sakcha. Yo awastha lai Rh asammanjasta bhanincha.

question_en: Which is better: cups, tampons, or pads?
question_ne: Cup, tampon, ki pad kun ramro?
response_en: The choice between menstrual cups, tampons, and pads depends on personal preference, comfort, and lifestyle. Menstrual cups are reusable and eco-friendly, tampons are discreet and convenient, and pads are non-invasive and easy to use. It's best to try different options to see which works best for you.
response_ne: Menstrual cup, tampon, ra pad ko bich ko chayan byakti gat ruchee, aram, ra jivan shaili ma nirbhar garcha. Menstrual cup haru punah prayog garna milne ra paryavaran mitra hunchan, tampon haru lukichupik ra sajilo hunchan, ra pad haru asani sanga prayog garna milcha. Tapai ko lagi kun ramro ho bhanera herna ko lagi bibhinna vikalpa haru prayog garnu ramro hunchha.

question_en: Why do periods happen only to girls?
question_ne: Mahawari kina keti haru lai matra huncha?
response_en: Periods occur in individuals with female reproductive systems. The menstrual cycle is a natural process preparing the body for potential pregnancy. Since males do not have the same reproductive organs, they do not experience menstruation.
response_ne: Mahina ko din mahila ko prajanan tantra(reproductive system) bhayeka byakti haru ma huncha. Menstrual cycle ek prakritik prakriya ho, jo sharir lai sambhavit garbhavastha ko lagi tayar garcha. Purush haru sanga yo prajanan angaharu hudaina, tesaile tiniharu lai mahina ko din hudaina.

question_en: Is it normal to have a headache during periods?
question_ne: Mahina ko din ma tauko dukhnu sadharan ho?
response_en: Yes, experiencing headaches during periods is common due to hormonal changes. However, if the headaches are severe or persistent, it's advisable to consult a healthcare professional.
response_ne: Ho, mahina ko din ma tauko dukhai hormonal pariwartan ko karanle sadharan ho. Tara, yadi tauko dukhai dherai chha ya dirghakalin chha bhane, swasthya biseshagya sanga paramarsha garnu upayukta hunchha.

question_en: I am still bleeding after 10 days of period.
question_ne: Mahina ko din suru bhayeko 10 din pachi pani bleeding bhairako cha.
response_en: Bleeding for more than 7 days is considered prolonged. It's important to consult a healthcare provider to determine the cause and receive appropriate treatment.
response_ne: 7 din bhanda badhi bleeding lambaeko manincha. Kaaran thaha pauna ra upayukta upachar pauna doctors sanga paramarsha garnu jaruri chha.

question_en: vomiting in period, is it normal?
question_ne: period ma vomiting normal ho?
response_en: Some individuals may experience nausea or vomiting during periods due to hormonal fluctuations. If this is severe or unusual for you, it's best to consult a healthcare professional.
response_ne: Kehi vyakti haru lai hormonal pariwartan ko karanle mahina ko din ma waak waak ya banta auna sakcha. Yadi yo dherai chha ya tapai ko lagi asadharan chha bhane, doctors sanga paramarsha garnu ramro hunchha.

question_en: Is vomiting normal during periods?

response_ne: Kehi vyakti haru lai hormonal pariwartan ko karanle mahina ko din ma waak waak ya banta auna sakcha. Yadi yo dherai chha ya tapai ko lagi asadharan chha bhane, doctors sanga paramarsha garnu ramro hunchha.

question_en: How can I delay my period?
question_ne: Mahina ko din kasari dhila garna sakchu?
response_en: Delaying your period can be achieved through hormonal medications, such as birth control pills. It's essential to consult with a healthcare provider before attempting to alter your menstrual cycle.
response_ne: Mahina ko din dhila garna hormonal aushadhi, jastai birth control pills, ko madat le sambhav chha. Afnai menstrual cycle pariwartan garnu agadi doctors sanga paramarsha garnu jaruri chha.

question_en: What are diseases related to the uterus?
question_ne: uterus sanga sambandhit rog or disease haru ke ke hun?
response_en: Diseases related to the uterus include fibroids, endometriosis, uterine polyps, and uterine cancer. If you experience unusual symptoms, it's important to consult a healthcare professional.
response_ne: Uterus sanga sambandhit rog haru ma fibroids, endometriosis, uterine polyps, ra bachchadani ko cancer sammilit chan. Yadi tapai lai asadharan lakshan haru dekhincha bhane, doctors sanga consult garnu hola.

question_en: My period blood is black, is it normal?
question_ne: Mero mahina ko ragat kalo cha. Yo ko ho?
response_en: Black period blood is usually old blood that has taken longer to exit the uterus. It's typically seen at the beginning or end of a period and is generally not a cause for concern.
response_ne: Kalo mahina ko ragat purano ragat ho, jasle garera bachha dhanibata niskina dhilo bhayo. Yo sadharanataya mahina ko suru ma ya antim ma dekhincha ra samanaya chinta ko karan hudaina.

question_en: My period blood is gray. What does this mean?
question_ne: Mero mahina ko ragat khairo cha. Yo ko ho?
response_en: Gray period blood can be a sign of bacterial infection, especially if accompanied by a foul odor or other symptoms. It's important to consult a healthcare provider in this case.
response_ne: Khairo mahina ko ragat bacterial sankraman ko sanket huna sakcha, bises gari yadi durgandh ya anya lakshan sanga cha bhane. Yo avastha ma swasthya sewak sanga paramarsha garnu mahatwapurna cha.

question_en: My period blood is orange. What does this mean?
question_ne: Mero mahina ko ragat suntale cha. Yo ko ho?
response_en: Orange period blood may indicate blood mixed with cervical fluids. If it has a foul smell or is accompanied by discomfort, it could be a sign of infection, and you should consult a healthcare provider.
response_ne: Suntale mahina ko ragat garbhashaya ko padarth sanga misieko ragat ko sanket huna sakcha. Yadi yo ma durgandh cha ya asajilo sanga cha bhane, yo sankraman ko sanket huna sakcha, ra tapai le swasthya sewak sanga paramarsha garnu parcha.

response_en: Common side effects of these medications can include stomach upset, dizziness, or allergic reactions. It's important to follow the recommended dosage and consult a healthcare provider if you experience any adverse effects.

question_en: What is PMS?
question_ne: PMS ke ho?
response_en: PMS stands for premenstrual syndrome, which includes symptoms like mood swings, bloating, and headaches that occur before the menstrual period.
response_ne: PMS ko matlab premenstrual syndrome ho, jasle mood ko pariwartan, pet fulne, ra tauko dukhai jasta lakshan haru samel garcha, jo mahina aunu agadi dekhincha.

question_en: How long does PMS last?
question_ne: PMS kati din samma rahanchha?

response_ne: PMS ka lakshan samanaya rupma kehi din dekhi dui hapta samma mahina suru hunu agadi rahanchha.

question_en: What are the symptoms of PMS?
question_ne: PMS ka lakshan haru ke ke hun?
response_en: PMS symptoms include mood swings, tender breasts, food cravings, fatigue, irritability, and depression.
response_ne: PMS ka symptoms ma manasik pariwartan, breast dukhne, khana ko ichha badhne, thakai, ra sadness hun.

question_en: How to use tampons?
question_ne: Tampon kasari use garne?
response_en: Insert the tampon into the vagina carefully, following the instructions on the package. Change it every 4-6 hours.
response_ne: Tampon lai vagina ma ramrari halnu parcha, packet ma diyeko nirdeshan anusar. Harek 4-6 ghanta ma change garnu parcha.

question_en: How to use pads?
question_ne: Pad kasari use garne?
response_en: Peel off the adhesive strip and place the pad on your underwear, ensuring it covers the area where blood flows. Change it every 4-6 hours or as needed.
response_ne: Chipkine patta hataera, pad lai aafno underwear ma rakhnus, ragat bahane thau ma purai dhakos. Harek 4-6 ghanta ma ya awashyakta anusar change garnus.

question_en: How to use a menstrual cup?
question_ne: Menstrual cup kasari use garne?
response_en: Fold the menstrual cup and insert it into the vagina. It will open up and collect the blood. Empty and clean it every 8-12 hours.
response_ne: Menstrual cup lai moadhera vagina ma halnus. Yo khulera ragat collect garne cha. Prati 8-12 ghanta ma khali ra sapha garnus.

question_en: What does different different blood colors mean?
question_ne: "Different different blood color ko meaning k ho?"
response_en: Dark red/maroon: Normal- regular period blood. Bright red: Fresh blood- usually a healthy flow. Black or brown: Old blood that took longer to exit- normal. Dark purple: Could indicate hormonal imbalance or endometriosis. Orange or gray: Might indicate infection- consult a doctor!
response_ne: "Gahiro ratto (Dark red/maroon): normal period ko blood ho. Halukko ratto (Bright red): Naya blood- normal. Kalo ya khairo (Black or brown): Pura safai na bhayeko purano ragat ho- normal ho. Gahiro jamuni ya purple (Dark purple): Hormonal imbalance ya endometriosis huna sakcha. Narani (Orange) ya khasro (Gray): Infection huna sakcha- doctor sanga kura garnu!"
